link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes === link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes === link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes === link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes === link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes === link|Q11397966}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Soegawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Takaokayama Santyô.JPG|250px]]
The inner shrine enshrined near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}{{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}} Uraōmachi Aza Kosaka 45 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|39|58|24|N|140|05|16|E|region:JP-05_type:landmark_scale:30000|display=inline,title|name=Soegawa Shrine}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha (small, disputed), Gōsha |Founded = Around the {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090|1=6th century}} |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=July 1|Q2700|WD=Q2700|1=July 1}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|label=Soegawa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=240}} }} '''Soegawa Shrine''' (そえがわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located near the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} in {{ill|lt=Hachirogata Town|Q1205526|WD=Q1205526|1=Hachirōgata, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Minamiakita District|Q1155694|WD=Q1155694|1=Minamiakita District, Akita}}, {{ill|lt=Akita Prefecture|Q81863|WD=Q81863|1=Akita Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small shrine in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (disputed), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === During the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it is said to have been a sacred site of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|WD=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} that enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Kubota Domain|Q1067720|WD=Q1067720|lt=Akita Domain|1=Kubota Domain}}, carried out the restoration of ancient shrines within the domain, this site was identified as the location of the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine,” leading to its revival. Throughout the Edo period, as one of the three principal shrines of the Akita Domain, it held a shrine estate of 30 koku. Originally, {{ill|lt=Yamamoto District|Q1155896|WD=Q1155896|1=Yamamoto District, Akita}} referred to what is now {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q733727|WD=Q733727|1=Senboku District, Akita}}. Plates excavated from the {{ill|lt=Hotta Fort Ruins|Q11497278|WD=Q11497278|1=Hotta-no-saku}} also bear the inscription “Yamamoto.” There is a theory that, in order to identify the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” with the present Soegawa Shrine, an area formerly called “Hiyama District” was renamed “Yamamoto District.” Originally, {{ill|lt=Mount Takaoka (Akita Prefecture)|Q17225894|WD=Q17225894|lt=Mount Takaoka|1=Mount Takaoka}} had a shrine called Kōmine Shrine, whose enshrined deity was {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}. According to records, when the Kubota Domain was asked by the Yoshida family of Kyoto in {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} about the existence of Soegawa Shrine, the domain’s shrine magistrate, Mogi Tomonori, regarded Mount Takaoka as a sacred site and established Soegawa Shrine by jointly enshrining Kōmine Shrine. Local elders say, “This place is called Hatarazawa and also Horoha-yama; though one mountain, it has eight valleys.” The *Gun’yūki* records, “It may be the main sanctuary of Mount Horoha.” Soegawa is also read as “Soikawa.”*The Gods and Shrines of Akita*, by Hisaji Satō, Akita Shinshū Research Association, 1981 [[File:Takaokayama Zyoyatô.JPG|thumb|right|Mount Takaoka – Soegawa Shrine stone lantern]] The stone lantern at the third torii gate on the mountainside (commonly called the middle torii) was a permanent lantern approximately 2.5 meters tall. In the past, it served as a lighthouse for boats traveling across Hachirōgata Lagoon. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was designated a Gōsha. === Northernmost Shikinaisha === In terms of its present location, it is the northernmost Shikinaisha in Japan. However, according to shrine tradition based on the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, the original site of Soegawa Shrine was the present {{ill|lt=Take Rokushō Shrine|Q11477429|WD=Q11477429|1=Dake Rokusho Shrine}}. As this view is supported by {{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} and many local historians, the shrine that should be considered the northernmost in its original location is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiga Riwake Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326|1=Iwate Prefecture}}. The disputed shrines for the Shikinaisha “Yamamoto District, one-seat small Soegawa Shrine” include this shrine, the aforementioned Take Rokushō Shrine, its village shrine Hachiman Shrine, and Soikawa Shinmei Shrine (present-day Soikawa, {{ill|lt=Akita City|Q17139|WD=Q17139|1=Akita (city)}}, Akita Prefecture). === Enshrined deities === {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} ({{ill|lt=Uke Mochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|lt=Uke Mochi-no-Ōkami|1=Ukemochi}}*Akita Encyclopedia*, edited by {{ill|lt=Akita Sakigake Shimpo|Q11595779|WD=Q11595779|1=Akita Sakigake Shimpō}}, 1981, ISBN 4870200074), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Ōkami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Chronology === 〈〉 indicates related events * Around {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}} ({{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1): Founded upon a petition by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|Q177517|WD=Q177517|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and others * {{ill|lt=871|Q24754|WD=Q24754|1=871}} ({{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 13): Granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade * {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7): 〈{{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinobu|Q1071525|WD=Q1071525|1=Satake Yoshinobu}} enters Akita〉 * {{ill|lt=1714|Q6878|WD=Q6878|1=1714}} ({{ill|lt=Shōtoku|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 4): According to shrine tradition, relocated to the present site and restored by {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshinori|Q8514533|WD=Q8514533|1=Satake Yoshitada}} * March {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1): 〈{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu bunri edict|1=UNKNOWN}}〉 * May 14, {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4): 〈Establishment of the {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}}〉 * {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5): Listed as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} * {{ill|lt=1899|Q7851|WD=Q7851|1=1899}}–{{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 32–33): A villager surnamed Yoshida discovered numerous octagonal mirrors while digging lakebed mud with a hoe. One, a Zuika Sōhō mirror from the Heian period, was preserved at Soegawa ShrineIsoo Ōba, *Studies in Shinto Archaeology*, 1943, p. 398. * {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43): Uraōmachi Shinmei Shrine jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44): Kodachibana Inari Shrine jointly enshrined === Access === * About 4 km from {{ill|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata Interchange|Q10881646|WD=Q10881646|lt=Gojōme-Hachirōgata IC|1=Gojome-Hachirogata interchange}} ** Via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 15|Q11595570|WD=Q11595570|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 15}}, {{ill|lt=National Route 7|Q1055550|WD=Q1055550|1=Japan National Route 7}}, and {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 220|Q11595607|WD=Q11595607|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 220}} * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Ōu Main Line|Q386495|WD=Q386495|1=Ōu Main Line}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Hachirōgata Station|Q5637087|WD=Q5637087|1=Hachirōgata Station}} via {{ill|lt=Akita Prefectural Route 219|Q11595605|WD=Q11595605|1=Akita Prefectural Road Route 219}} ** About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Koikawa Station|Q6426274|WD=Q6426274|1=Koikawa Station (Akita)}} via National Route 7 === Literary works === * Hokumei Kitajima, *Soegawa Reigen Dewagoya: New Jōruri*, Shiratsuchi Kōriki, 1914 === Notes ===
